context.p: The tcpconnect utility uses eBPF features to trace outgoing TCP connection attempts. The output of the utility also includes connections that failed.
The tcpconnect utility is lightweight because it traces, for example, the connect() function of the kernel instead of capturing packets and filtering them.

step.cmd: Enter the following command to start the tracing process that displays all outgoing connections:

info.codeblock: # /usr/share/bcc/tools/tcpconnect PID COMM IP SADDR DADDR DPORT 31346 curl 4 192.0.2.1 198.51.100.16 80 31348 telnet 4 192.0.2.1 203.0.113.231 23 31361 isc-worker00 4 192.0.2.1 192.0.2.254 53 ...
info.p: Each time the kernel processes an outgoing connection, tcpconnect displays the details of the connections.

step.cmd: Press Ctrl+C to stop the tracing process.
